weather_line: Clear over Diagon Alley · the Subcommittee is not

stories.headline: Christine's Written Response Arrives at Eight Forty-Three; Close of Business Was Five O'Clock; Griphook the Younger Has Not Yet Determined Whether This Is Early or Simply a Different Definition of Thursday
stories.byline: By our Gringotts correspondent, filing from the corridor
body: The Senior Audit Subcommittee's written response deadline — close of business Thursday, as specified verbally by Griphook the Younger at the conclusion of Wednesday's session — was rendered technically ambiguous at eight forty-three this morning, when Christine's response arrived, bound, tabbed, cross-referenced, and accompanied by a one-page executive summary the Subcommittee had not requested but is now contractually obligated to acknowledge, having accepted the document.

The response is eleven pages. It addresses every item in the audit log, including the two inaccuracies corrected on Tuesday, the disputed fifth entry, and a previously unlogged incident from the third week of June that the Subcommittee was not aware it was investigating. Appendix C contains the Duty Goblin's original log, annotated in three colours. Appendix D is a blank page labelled 'Space Reserved for Subcommittee Findings.' It has been left generously wide.

By nine fifteen, the Subcommittee had convened informally in the antechamber. By nine thirty-two, Griphook the Younger had moved that the Inquiry be formally concluded with a finding of 'No Deficiencies Identified in the Liaison Wing; Inquiry Scope to Be Reviewed.' The vote was five to zero. The log's final entry, written in Christine's hand at eight forty-two — one minute before delivery — reads: 'Inquiry complete. Efficiency confirmed. Have a good Thursday.' The Duty Goblin has not placed a DISPUTED marker beside it. He has been observed, once, to nod.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Thursday Bulletin Confirming Thursday; Notes That the Efficiency Inquiry Has Concluded; Describes the Outcome as 'Consistent With Prior Expectations,' Which Is Not What It Said on Monday
body: The Ministry of Magic's Thursday bulletin, distributed at ten o'clock via enchanted interoffice memo, confirms that Thursday is occurring on schedule and that the Gringotts Senior Audit Subcommittee has concluded its review of the Liaison Wing with a finding the bulletin characterises as 'broadly anticipated.' Monday's bulletin, which described the audit as 'a timely and necessary review of systemic patterns,' was not attached. Requests for the Monday bulletin have been redirected to a sub-office that does not appear on the building directory.

In a footnote, the bulletin notes that Appendix D of the Subcommittee's received documentation 'raises questions the Ministry is prepared to answer but has chosen, on reflection, not to at this time.' The blank page has been filed under Exhibit E. The Ministry's photograph this week is of Chamber Eleven, empty, taken from the doorway. Christine is not in it. Her chair is.

stories.headline: Kitchen Table, Having Passed Through Northeastern Chart, Coffee Cup, and Mail, Now Hosts Nothing at All; Experts Call This a Phase; the Table Appears Unbothered
body: The kitchen table, which served as operational headquarters for the Duck Bandit investigation for the better part of a fortnight before hosting a transitional arrangement of one coffee cup and several pieces of forwarded mail, was observed Thursday morning in a state of what analysts are describing as 'open readiness.' Nothing is on it. This has not happened since the second week of June.

Steve, asked whether the table's current condition represents an ending or a beginning, declined to answer directly and instead refilled his coffee. The table, for its part, has been noted to catch the morning light well. The enchanted Jeep, parked outside, is also unbothered. It forgave him again last night, as it does.

stories.headline: Jersey's Supplementary Brief Accepted by Full Household Tribunal; a New Precedent Is Set; the Precedent Is Immediately Slept Upon
body: The warmest-lap ruling, now in its fifth consecutive day, achieved new legal standing Thursday morning when Jersey's supplementary brief — a nap of some forty minutes, delivered directly atop the relevant documentation — was accepted without objection by the assembled household. Legal scholars note that acceptance by non-objection is a recognised standard. Jersey noted nothing, being asleep.

In a related development, Ellie was observed this morning attempting to befriend a sunbeam moving across the floor. She followed it for eleven minutes before it disappeared behind the sofa. She has checked behind the sofa twice since. Lucy watched from the doorway and offered no assistance, which is consistent with her established character and, some argue, her alibi for several other recent matters.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was seen near the kitchen table at eight forty-one this morning — one minute before Christine dispatched the Subcommittee response. The timing has been noted. Lucy has declined to confirm or deny whether she proofread Appendix C.

Ellie: Spent the better part of the morning in scientific pursuit of a moving sunbeam and has now concluded, with great confidence, that sunbeams live behind the sofa. She will check again after lunch, in case one has returned.
Jersey: The supplementary brief has been filed, accepted, and is currently being extended into a second volume. Jersey considers this productivity. The lap agrees.
founding_editor_note: Eleven pages, tabbed, with a blank appendix. I built these presses level and square, and I am telling you: that blank page did more work than the other ten combined. — D.

in_brief.sport: Gringotts Subcommittee lifts its own motion; Christine's deadlift total for the week, by contrast, remains uncontested and unaudited.
in_brief.forecast: Clear skies through the afternoon · a new arc is forming on the horizon, direction as yet unnamed · the brass ducks face the door.

storyline_updates.title: The Gringotts Efficiency Inquiry
storyline_updates.summary: Resolved Thursday morning. Christine's response arrived at eight forty-three — before close of business by any reasonable definition, and before most of the Subcommittee had finished their morning tea. Eleven pages, tabbed, with a blank Appendix D labelled 'Space Reserved for Subcommittee Findings.' The Subcommittee voted five to zero to conclude with a finding of no deficiencies in the Liaison Wing. The log's final entry is Christine's. The Duty Goblin did not dispute it. Arc closed with full honours and a generously wide margin.

storyline_updates.title: The Table Awaits
storyline_updates.summary: New arc, opening quietly: the kitchen table is empty for the first time since mid-June. It has hosted a goblin-trail investigation, a transitional coffee cup, and a handful of mail. It is now clear. Something is coming — a new expedition, a new project, a new plan. Steve has not said what. The Jeep's tank, sources note, is full. Day 2 should bring the first hint of what the table is about to host: a map, a set of tools, a waymarker circled in pencil, or something Steve produces from the garage that requires a flat surface and some thought.
